hero_subtitle: SFTY Business streamlines jurisdiction-specific compliance workflows by connecting multi-provider outputs into one operational layer for audit-ready cross-border oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: VASPs struggle to manage multiple provider outputs and jurisdiction-specific policies in disconnected workflows. This fragmentation slows onboarding, complicates Travel Rule exception handling, and weakens the audit trail needed for regulatory review across borders.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates provider outputs, jurisdictional rules, Travel Rule exceptions and case workflows into one unified compliance layer that streamlines onboarding, audit evidence and regulatory coordination across borders.
workflow_title: From cross-border checks to compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY orchestrates provider outputs across jurisdictions, applying local rules and policies to route reviews, open cases, preserve evidence and support compliance decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another provider. Jurisdictional workflow layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects cross-border compliance outputs into coordinated workflows, cases, evidence, and audit-ready reporting to manage multi-jurisdictional regulatory complexity for VASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing multi-jurisdiction workflows with controlled evidence, bank due diligence and enterprise-grade deployment for consistent, auditable compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support cross-border compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific rules, Travel Rule requirements and provider outputs into one workflow, enabling multi-jurisdiction compliance for VASPs handling cross-border digital asset transactions.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY orchestrate multiple providers?
faq_2_answer: SFTY consolidates outputs from KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, applying policies and routing reviews to create connected, auditable compliance cases across multiple jurisdictions.
faq_3_question: How is audit-ready evidence preserved in SFTY?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case evidence and provider outputs in structured audit trails to support regulatory inspections and defend compliance decisions across borders.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with existing onboarding, KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening systems without disruption, layering a structured compliance workflow and governance framework on top.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into connected workflows, decisions, evidence and reporting supporting regulated digital asset businesses.
final_title: Connect cross-border provider outputs into cases
final_description: Review how SFTY structures multi-jurisdiction compliance data into audit-ready cases, decisions and regulatory rule workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
